content.problemStatement:
There are NNN integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč on a circle. For each valid iii, AiA_iAi‚Äč and Ai+1A_{i+1}Ai+1‚Äč are adjacent; A1A_1A1‚Äč and ANA_NAN‚Äč are also adjacent.

Chef has a sequence of integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč, which he may use to perform any number of operations (including zero). In one operation, he should do the following:


Choose an integer iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N).
Choose iii consecutive integers on the circle.
Choose a sign S=¬Ī1S = \pm 1S=¬Ī1 and add S‚čÖBiS \cdot B_iS‚čÖBi‚Äč to each of the chosen integers. In other words, either add BiB_iBi‚Äč to each of them or subtract BiB_iBi‚Äč from each of them.
The goal is to make all the integers on the circle equal to 000. Can Chef achieve it using these operations?
constraints: 0‚Č§Ai,Bi‚Č§1040 \le A_i, B_i \le 10^40‚Č§Ai‚Äč,Bi‚Äč‚Č§104 for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10410^4104
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"YES"` if it is possible to make all integers on the circle equal to 000 or `"NO"` if it is impossible.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: We can make everything 000 using two operations: choose i=4i = 4i=4 and change the circular sequence (1,1,3,3,2)(1, 1, 3, 3, 2)(1,1,3,3,2) to (1‚ąí1,1‚ąí1,3‚ąí1,3‚ąí1,2)=(0,0,2,2,2)(1-1, 1-1, 3-1, 3-1, 2) = (0, 0, 2, 2, 2)(1‚ąí1,1‚ąí1,3‚ąí1,3‚ąí1,2)=(0,0,2,2,2), then choose i=3i = 3i=3 and change it to (0,0,2‚ąí2,2‚ąí2,2‚ąí2)=(0,0,0,0,0)(0, 0, 2-2, 2-2, 2-2) = (0, 0, 0, 0, 0)(0,0,2‚ąí2,2‚ąí2,2‚ąí2)=(0,0,0,0,0).
Example case 2: The parities of integers on the circle cannot change, so they cannot become 000.
